target.title: fix(ui): prevent skill toggle state transfer with Lit repeat directive [AI-assisted]
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem**: When a skill toggle is flipped in the Skills view (e.g., from "Ready" tab), the toggled skill moves to a different status tab. The checkbox checked state bleeds into the next skill that takes its place. Issue #89661.
- **Root Cause**: The skills list is rendered using Array.map() without stable DOM keys. When a skill moves to another group/visibility after toggle, Lit reuses the DOM element for the next skill. The checkbox .checked binding retains its previous value because Lit s change detection sees the same DOM node, not a new one.
- **Fix**: Replace Array.map() with Lit s repeat() directive keyed by group.id for group containers and skill.skillKey for individual skill items. This ensures each skill has a stable DOM identity across re-renders regardless of positional changes.
- **What changed**: ui/src/ui/views/skills.ts — imported repeat from lit/directives/repeat.js; replaced 2 Array.map() calls (groups + skills) with repeat() with stable key functions. ui/src/ui/views/skills.test.ts — added focused regression test verifying checkbox state is preserved per skill.skillKey when skills reorder across status tabs.
- **What did NOT change**: No behavioral or visual changes. Skill toggle functionality, status tabs, group rendering layout, and all other components remain identical.

## Verification

- skills.test.ts: 5/5 tests pass (including new regression test)
- oxfmt --check passes
- New test preserves checkbox state per skill when skills reorder across status tabs directly exercises the status-tab DOM reuse scenario from #89661

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Skill checkbox toggle state incorrectly transfers to the skill that visually replaces the toggled skill when it moves between status tabs (Ready to Disabled, etc.)
- **Real environment tested**: Windows 10 Pro, Node v24.16.0, Vitest v4.1.7 with jsdom
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: pnpm exec vitest run ui/src/ui/views/skills.test.ts --reporter=verbose
- **Evidence after fix**: 5 tests passed (1 new regression test), 0 failures. oxfmt --check clean. The new regression test renders skills across multiple status tab transitions and verifies that checkbox .checked state is correctly bound to each skill.skillKey regardless of positional changes.
- **Observed result after fix**: The repeat() directive with stable keys prevents Lit from reusing DOM elements when skills shift between groups or visibility states. Each skill s checkbox state is independently managed by its data (skill.disabled), not by DOM position.
- **What was not tested**: Full browser E2E screenshot or recording of the Control UI Skills tab (no running gateway available in this environment). The repeat() directive is a standard Lit pattern for this exact class of bug, and the jsdom regression test validates the rendering contract. A maintainer can request a visual proof via Mantis if needed.

Fixes #89661
